commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Hundreds. Josephus only allows David 4000 men. But his army seems to have been pretty numerous, since he divides it into three parts, and appoints three head commanders, with officers of a thousand, under them. See ver. 4. (Calmet)
He designed to lead them to battle himself, if the people had not dissuaded him. (Salien)
